# Serhii Shramko — Senior Frontend Engineer

> Personal portfolio and blog of Serhii Shramko, a Senior Software Engineer specializing in frontend development with React, TypeScript, and Next.js. Built with Next.js, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. Open source.

This site contains 35 blog posts on web development topics and 43 production-ready code snippets. Content is written in Markdown with MDX support, featuring rich frontmatter metadata including descriptions, keywords, and categories. The site also showcases browser extensions and developer tools.

## Pages

- [About](https://shramko.dev/about): Professional experience, skills, and background
- [Blog](https://shramko.dev/blog): Articles on React, TypeScript, Next.js, and web development
- [Snippets](https://shramko.dev/snippets): Production-ready code snippets for common tasks
- [Dashboard](https://shramko.dev/dashboard): Real-time GitHub and development stats
- [Gear](https://shramko.dev/gear): Development hardware and software setup
- [Bookmarks](https://shramko.dev/bookmarks): Curated developer bookmarks
- [Learning](https://shramko.dev/learning): Learning resources and progress

## Projects

- [Quizlet QuickList](https://shramko.dev/quizlet-list): Chrome extension for creating and managing Quizlet study lists
- [Udemy Progress Reset](https://shramko.dev/udemy-reset-progress): Chrome extension to reset progress on Udemy courses

## Blog Posts: React

- [Flexbox Grid in React: A Step-by-Step Guide](https://shramko.dev/blog/react-flexbox-grid): Learn how to build a reusable flexbox-based Grid component in React using BEM methodology and clsx for clean class management.
  Keywords: react flexbox, flexbox grid, CSS flexbox, responsive grid, React layout, clsx
- [React Elements, Children as Props, and Re-Renders](https://shramko.dev/blog/react-elements-children): Learn how React elements work and how passing children as props can optimize performance by preventing unnecessary re-renders.
  Keywords: react elements, children prop, react performance, re-renders, react optimization, composition
- [React Re-Renders: What Triggers Them and Why](https://shramko.dev/blog/react-rerender): Dive into the mechanics of React re-renders — learn what causes them, how they impact performance, and how to manage them effectively.
  Keywords: react re-render, what triggers re-render in react, react performance, react optimization, useEffect and re-render, rendering lifecycle react
- [Understanding the Hidden Pitfalls of Custom Hooks in React](https://shramko.dev/blog/react-hooks-pitfalls): Learn about performance risks in React custom hooks, how they cause unexpected re-renders, and strategies to manage state efficiently.
  Keywords: custom hooks, react re-render, react state management, react performance, hooks best practices, avoiding unnecessary renders

## Blog Posts: TypeScript

- [Discriminated Unions in TypeScript: Write Safer Code](https://shramko.dev/blog/discriminated-unions): Discover how discriminated unions in TypeScript help you write cleaner, safer code. Learn what they are, how to use them, and why they're great!
  Keywords: TypeScript Discriminated Unions, Tagged Unions, Sum Types, Type Safety, Exhaustiveness Checking
- [ESLint with TypeScript: Setup and Configuration Guide](https://shramko.dev/blog/eslint-with-typescript): Step-by-step guide to configuring ESLint with TypeScript. Set up linting rules, integrate with your IDE, and catch errors before they reach production.
  Keywords: TypeScript, ESLint, Project Setup, Optimizing TypeScript Development
- [What is a generic type parameter T in TypeScript?](https://shramko.dev/blog/generic-type-parameters): Learn TypeScript generic type parameter naming conventions. Understand when to use T, K, V and when descriptive names like TKey make your code more readable.
  Keywords: TypeScript generics, generic type parameters, TypeScript T parameter, generic naming conventions, generic constraints

## Blog Posts: JavaScript

- [Dispatch Tables in JavaScript: A Clean Code Approach](https://shramko.dev/blog/dispatch-tables): Replace messy switch statements with dispatch tables in JavaScript. Learn this clean code pattern for handling multiple conditions with maintainable code.
  Keywords: Switch statement in javascript, Dynamic Dispatch, Dispatch Tables, Conditionals in JavaScript, Polymorphism
- [Early Return Pattern in JavaScript — Guard Clauses Guide](https://shramko.dev/blog/the-early-return-pattern-in-javascript): Learn the early return pattern (guard clauses) in JavaScript and TypeScript. Reduce nesting, improve readability, and write cleaner functions with real-world examples.
  Keywords: early return javascript, guard clauses, javascript reduce nesting, early return vs nested if, fail fast pattern
- [JavaScript Arrays: Essential Methods for Beginners](https://shramko.dev/blog/js-arrays): Master JavaScript array methods with this beginner-friendly guide. Learn map, filter, reduce, find, and other essential methods with practical examples.
  Keywords: javascript arrays, javascript array replace, js array concat, js array includes, array methods
- [JavaScript Expressions vs Statements: What's the Difference?](https://shramko.dev/blog/expressions-statements): Learn the difference between expressions and statements in JavaScript. Understanding this distinction helps you write better code and avoid syntax errors.
  Keywords: JavaScript expressions, JavaScript statements, expression vs statement, JavaScript basics
- [JavaScript Loops: for...in vs for...of](https://shramko.dev/blog/for-in-vs-for-of): Understand the key differences between for...in and for...of loops in JavaScript. Learn when to use each for iterating over arrays, objects, and iterables.
  Keywords: for...in loop, for...of loop, JavaScript loops, iteration methods
- [JavaScript Naming Conventions: A Complete Guide for Clean Code](https://shramko.dev/blog/javascript-naming-conventions): Master JavaScript naming conventions with this guide. Learn best practices for naming variables, functions, and classes to write cleaner code.
  Keywords: javascript naming conventions, javascript best practices, clean code, variable naming, coding standards

## Blog Posts: Next.js & Frameworks

- [Astro Framework Guide: Build Faster Sites in 2026](https://shramko.dev/blog/astro): Master the Astro framework with this hands-on guide. Islands Architecture, Content Collections, View Transitions, and client directives with code examples.
  Keywords: astro framework, what is astro, astro islands architecture, astro client directives, astro content collections
- [Build a Link Tree with Astro.js and Vercel (2026 Guide)](https://shramko.dev/blog/linktree): Build a custom link tree with Astro.js and deploy it on Vercel. Step-by-step tutorial with TypeScript, analytics setup, and performance optimization tips.
  Keywords: build link tree Astro, Astro Vercel deploy, custom link tree website, Astro static site tutorial
- [Fix: Could not find Sharp in Astro.js (Vercel Deploy)](https://shramko.dev/blog/astro-image-on-vercel): Fix the [MissingSharp] Could not find Sharp error when deploying Astro.js to Vercel. Step-by-step Sharp image service setup and configuration guide.
  Keywords: MissingSharp Astro, Could not find Sharp, Astro image Vercel, Astro Sharp configuration
- [How to Use Shiki Class-Based Themes with next-mdx-remote in Next.js](https://shramko.dev/blog/shiki-class-based-themes-nextjs-mdx): Replace Shiki's bloated inline dual-theme styles with class-based CSS extraction using transformerStyleToClass and next-mdx-remote in Next.js.
  Keywords: Shiki dark mode Next.js, transformerStyleToClass, next-mdx-remote syntax highlighting, Shiki dual theme class CSS
- [I Fixed the Wrong Thing and Found the Real Bug](https://shramko.dev/blog/build-time-lies): I spotted a build regression, shipped what I thought was the fix, and the numbers didn't move. Turns out the metrics were lying too — Next.js reports batch timing, not page timing.
  Keywords: Next.js build performance, build time regression, Shiki highlighting performance, static generation batch timing
- [Presenting the Revamped shramko.dev: Explore the Exciting Updates!](https://shramko.dev/blog/introducing-the-new-shramko.dev): A deep dive into building my developer portfolio with Next.js 16, React 19, and Tailwind CSS 4. Learn about the tech stack, design decisions, and features behind 900+ commits.
  Keywords: Website Redesign, Next.js, React, Tailwind, Developer Portfolio

## Blog Posts: Node.js & Tooling

- [Fix "__dirname Not Defined" in ES Modules – Quick Guide](https://shramko.dev/blog/dirname-error): Fix the "__dirname is not defined in ES module scope" error in Node.js. Recreate __dirname and __filename using import.meta.url.
  Keywords: __dirname Node.js, ES modules, import.meta.url
- [Mastering npm and Semantic Versioning for Smarter Development](https://shramko.dev/blog/npm-semantic-versioning): A friendly, practical guide to SemVer in npm. Learn versioning rules, avoid dependency chaos, and manage updates with confidence.
  Keywords: npm semantic versioning, SemVer guide, package.json versioning, caret vs tilde npm
- [Migrate from npm to pnpm – Save Time with This Guide](https://shramko.dev/blog/pnpm): Migrate from npm to pnpm and cut your deployment time by 44%. Step-by-step guide covering installation, lockfile conversion, and CI/CD configuration.
  Keywords: migrate npm to pnpm, pnpm migration guide, pnpm benefits, npm alternatives
- [Node Version Manager (NVM): Install and Switch Versions](https://shramko.dev/blog/nvm): Learn how to install and use Node Version Manager (NVM) to switch between Node.js versions effortlessly. Manage multiple Node versions for different projects.
  Keywords: what is nvm, install nvm, nvm set default node version
- [Use Postgres Connect URLs for DB Connections](https://shramko.dev/blog/postgres-connect-url): Learn how to connect to PostgreSQL using a connection URL string. Simple guide for Node.js, DataGrip, or any client supporting URL-based database connections.
  Keywords: database connection url, postgres connect url, how to connect to database

  Keywords: SSH Keys, GitHub Account, Git Terminal, Generating SSH Key, ed25519
- [Conventional Commits enhance your coding practices](https://shramko.dev/blog/conventional-commits): Master Conventional Commits to write clear, structured commit messages. Learn the format, types like feat and fix, and how to automate changelog generation.
  Keywords: Conventional Commits, commit messages, JavaScript workflow, changelog generation
- [When .gitignore Isn't the Right Tool](https://shramko.dev/blog/gitignore-is-not-enough): Learn about Git's hidden alternatives to .gitignore for managing file exclusions locally without affecting your team's workflow.
  Keywords: git info exclude, git assume unchanged, git local ignore, gitignore alternatives

## Snippets: TypeScript Patterns

- [Branded Types in TypeScript: Never Mix Up IDs Again](https://shramko.dev/snippets/branded-types): Use TypeScript branded types (nominal types) to prevent mixing up strings and numbers that represent different things. Stop userId/orderId bugs at compile time.
  Keywords: TypeScript branded types, nominal types, type branding, TypeScript ID types, type safety
- [DistributiveOmit Type for Union Types](https://shramko.dev/snippets/distributive-omit): Custom TypeScript utility type that properly distributes Omit across union type members. Fixes the standard Omit behavior with discriminated unions.
  Keywords: TypeScript utility types, distributive types, conditional types, Omit union types
- [Template Literal Types in TypeScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/template-literal-types): Create complex string types with TypeScript template literals. Useful for CSS-in-JS libraries, event systems, and strongly typed string patterns.
  Keywords: TypeScript template literal types, template literals, string types, CSS-in-JS type safety
- [TypeScript satisfies Operator: Keep Your Types Precise](https://shramko.dev/snippets/satisfies-operator): Learn when to use TypeScript's satisfies operator instead of type annotations. Validate object shapes while preserving literal types and autocomplete.
  Keywords: TypeScript satisfies, satisfies operator, TypeScript 4.9, type inference, literal types
- [TypeScript Type Predicates: Smart Type Guards for Safer Code](https://shramko.dev/snippets/type-predicates): Learn how to use TypeScript type predicates to create powerful type guards that help the compiler understand your code better.
  Keywords: TypeScript type predicates, type guards, type narrowing, is operator TypeScript, user-defined type guards

## Snippets: React Hooks

- [React Hook Tutorial: How to Use the useHasMounted Hook](https://shramko.dev/snippets/use-has-mounted): Avert strange SSR problems with this useful React hook. Identify whether your rendering occurs on the client or server!
  Keywords: React useEffect, conditional rendering, component lifecycle, custom React hooks, useState
- [useClickOutside React Hook: Detect Clicks Outside an Element](https://shramko.dev/snippets/use-click-outside): React hook to detect clicks outside a component. Close dropdowns, modals, and popovers when users click outside. TypeScript, zero dependencies.
  Keywords: useClickOutside, click outside React, close dropdown on click outside, close modal on click outside, React ref
- [useLocalStorage React Hook: Persist State Across Sessions](https://shramko.dev/snippets/use-local-storage): React hook for localStorage with TypeScript, cross-tab sync, and SSR support. Drop-in replacement for react-use or usehooks-ts. Uses useSyncExternalStore.
  Keywords: useLocalStorage, React localStorage hook, persist state React, useSyncExternalStore, cross-tab sync
- [useMediaQuery React Hook: Responsive Design in JavaScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/use-media-query): React hook for responsive breakpoints in JavaScript. Detect screen size changes, dark mode preference, and other CSS media queries in React components.
  Keywords: useMediaQuery, React responsive hook, media query JavaScript, detect screen size, window matchMedia
- [useToggle React Hook](https://shramko.dev/snippets/use-toggle-react-hook): Simple useToggle React hook for managing boolean state. Toggle between true and false with a clean API. Perfect for modals, dropdowns, and dark mode switches.
  Keywords: useToggle hook, Boolean toggle hook, React state management, useState toggle

## Snippets: JavaScript Utilities

- [Debounce snippet function in modern TypeScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/debounce): Copy-paste debounce function in TypeScript. Delay function execution until user stops typing or triggering events. Includes React usage example.
  Keywords: debounce, debounce function, debounce function in JavaScript, debounce function in TypeScript
- [Effective Ways to Remove Duplicates from Arrays in JavaScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/remove-duplicates-array): Three simple ways to remove duplicate values from JavaScript arrays. Learn to use Set, filter with indexOf, and reduce methods with clear code examples.
  Keywords: remove duplicates array, unique array, Set, filter indexOf, reduce
- [Group Array by Key in JavaScript and TypeScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/group-by): Group an array of objects by a property value in TypeScript. Covers Object.groupBy, Map.groupBy, and a manual fallback for older environments.
  Keywords: group array by key, groupBy JavaScript, Object.groupBy, Map.groupBy, array reduce group
- [How to Use the Range Function in JavaScript: A Simple Guide](https://shramko.dev/snippets/generate-range): Create an array of sequential numbers with a JavaScript range function. Similar to Python's range(), generates number sequences with optional step values.
  Keywords: JavaScript range function, generate sequence of numbers, JavaScript array creation, utility functions
- [JavaScript Deep Clone: How to Safely Copy Objects](https://shramko.dev/snippets/deep-clone): Create deep clones of JavaScript objects including nested structures. Copy-paste function using structuredClone for reliable object duplication.
  Keywords: deep clone, structuredClone, deep copy vs shallow copy, copy nested object
- [JavaScript Random Number Generation: Best Practices](https://shramko.dev/snippets/random): JavaScript function to generate random numbers within a range. Works with integers and floats. Copy-paste snippet using Math.random() for any project.
  Keywords: JavaScript getRandom function, random number generator, generate random float, Math.random
- [Sleep in JavaScript: How to Pause Execution](https://shramko.dev/snippets/sleep): Learn how to pause JavaScript execution with a sleep function. Create delays using Promises and async/await for timing control in your code.
  Keywords: sleep in JavaScript, pause execution, delay in JavaScript, sleep function
- [Sort an Array of Objects by Multiple Properties in JavaScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/sort-an-array-of-objects-in-javascript): Learn how to sort an array of objects by multiple properties in JavaScript using the Array.prototype.sort() method with a custom compare function.
  Keywords: JavaScript Array Object Sorting, Array.prototype.sort, custom compare function, sort by multiple properties
- [Throttle Function in TypeScript with React Example](https://shramko.dev/snippets/throttle): Copy-paste throttle function in TypeScript. Limit how often a function fires during scroll, resize, or mousemove events. Includes React usage example.
  Keywords: throttle, throttle function, throttle vs debounce, scroll throttle, rate limit function calls

## Snippets: DOM & Browser

- [Copy to Clipboard in JavaScript: Modern Async Clipboard API](https://shramko.dev/snippets/copy-to-clipboard): Copy text to clipboard using the modern Async Clipboard API in TypeScript. Includes a ready-to-use React copy button component with success feedback.
  Keywords: copy to clipboard JavaScript, clipboard API, navigator clipboard writeText, copy button React
- [CSS Scroll-Driven Animations: Replace JavaScript with Pure CSS](https://shramko.dev/snippets/scroll-driven-animations): Pure CSS scroll-linked animations with scroll() and view() timelines. Build progress bars, fade-ins, and parallax effects without a single line of JavaScript.
  Keywords: scroll-driven animations, CSS scroll animation, animation-timeline, scroll progress CSS, view timeline CSS
- [Generating UUIDs in JavaScript: Best Practices](https://shramko.dev/snippets/window-crypto): Generate UUIDs in JavaScript using window.crypto.randomUUID(). Copy-paste snippet for creating universally unique identifiers in browser environments.
  Keywords: window.crypto.randomUUID, UUID JavaScript, unique identifiers
- [JavaScript Caps Lock Detection | getModifierState](https://shramko.dev/snippets/detect-caps-lock): Detect Caps Lock state in JavaScript using getModifierState(). Copy-paste snippet for password fields and form validation with cross-browser support.
  Keywords: JavaScript Caps Lock detection, getModifierState, keyboard events, modifier keys, form validation
- [JavaScript Techniques for Checking Element Visibility in the Viewport](https://shramko.dev/snippets/is-element-in-viewport): Check if an element is visible in the viewport using JavaScript. Covers Intersection Observer API and manual methods for lazy loading and animations.
  Keywords: JavaScript viewport check, Intersection Observer API, visibility detection, lazy loading
- [Lazy Load Images in TypeScript Using Observer](https://shramko.dev/snippets/lazy-loading-images-with-intersection-observer): Complete guide to implementing lazy loading for images using Intersection Observer API with TypeScript, including code examples and best practices.
  Keywords: Intersection Observer, lazy loading, TypeScript, image optimization, web performance
- [How to Move Cursor to the End of Input Field with JavaScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/move-cursor): JavaScript snippet to programmatically move the text cursor to the end or beginning of an input field. Useful for form validation and auto-focus features.
  Keywords: move cursor to end of input, cursor position, setSelectionRange, input field focus

## Snippets: URL & String Validation

- [Check if URL is Absolute - JavaScript Function](https://shramko.dev/snippets/is-absolute-url): A JavaScript function that uses regex to determine if a given URL string is absolute by checking for http or https protocol prefix.
  Keywords: javascript url validation, absolute url, regex, http https, utility function
- [Efficient Methods to Validate Alphabetic Characters in JavaScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/check-if-character-is-a-letter-in-javascript): Check if a character is a letter in JavaScript using regex patterns and ASCII codes. Reliable methods for character validation including Unicode support.
  Keywords: JavaScript character validation, check if letter, regex letters, ASCII validation, Unicode character check
- [Email Validation in TypeScript – Regex Function](https://shramko.dev/snippets/is-email-valid-regex): TypeScript function for validating email addresses using regex. Copy-paste snippet with type safety for form validation in your web applications.
  Keywords: email validation, regular expression, regex, form validation, TypeScript function
- [How to Capitalize the First Letter of a String in JavaScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/how-to-capitalize-first-letter-of-string-in-javascript): Capitalize the first letter of a string in JavaScript. Copy-paste function with examples for toUpperCase, charAt, and slice string manipulation.
  Keywords: capitalize first letter, JavaScript string manipulation, toUpperCase, charAt
- [How to Get the Current URL with JavaScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/how-to-get-the-current-url-with-javascript): Learn different methods to retrieve the current URL using JavaScript for analytics tracking, dynamic content updates, and more.
  Keywords: current URL JavaScript, window.location, location.pathname, URLSearchParams, query parameters
- [Removing Query Strings from URLs in JavaScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/how-to-remove-query): Snippet to remove a query string from a URL using JavaScript. Learn how to remove query parameters from a URL string.
  Keywords: remove query string from URL, remove query parameters, JavaScript URL manipulation

  Keywords: CSS selection, ::selection pseudo-element, text selection styling, CSS UX

- [Making Elements Visually Hidden for Screen Readers and Accessibility](https://shramko.dev/snippets/visually-hidden): CSS snippet to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. Essential technique for web accessibility and WCAG compliance.
  Keywords: visually hidden CSS, accessibility, screen reader, WCAG compliance, aria-hidden

## Snippets: Data & Storage

- [How to Compare Dates in JavaScript: Check Date Ranges Easily](https://shramko.dev/snippets/compare-dates): JavaScript function to check if a date falls between two other dates. Uses getTime() for accurate comparison. Perfect for date validation and booking systems.
  Keywords: JS Date, check if date is between two dates, date getTime method
- [Intl.NumberFormat: Format Currency & Units in JavaScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/intl-numberformat): Format currency, percentages, units, and compact numbers in JavaScript with Intl.NumberFormat — one native API, zero dependencies, full locale support.
  Keywords: Intl.NumberFormat, format currency JavaScript, locale number formatting, i18n number format
- [Set Methods in JavaScript: union, intersection, difference & More](https://shramko.dev/snippets/set-methods): Use native Set methods — union, intersection, difference, symmetricDifference, isSubsetOf, isSupersetOf, and isDisjointFrom — in JavaScript. No polyfill needed.
  Keywords: Set methods JavaScript, Set union, Set intersection, Set difference, ES2024 Set methods
- [Type-Safe localStorage Wrapper in TypeScript](https://shramko.dev/snippets/local-storage): A type-safe localStorage wrapper with JSON parsing, error handling, and expiry support. Drop-in replacement for raw localStorage calls in TypeScript projects.
  Keywords: localStorage TypeScript, localStorage wrapper, type-safe localStorage, localStorage with expiry

  Keywords: check environment, check if code is running in browser, check if code is running in Node.js
- [How to check if a file exists in Node.js](https://shramko.dev/snippets/how-to-check-if-file-exists-node): Learn how to check if a file exists in Node.js using the fs module. Covers synchronous and asynchronous methods with practical examples.
  Keywords: nodejs file system, fs module, synchronous file check, file existence
- [How to Install PostgreSQL on macOS](https://shramko.dev/snippets/postgres-install-macos): Step-by-step guide to install PostgreSQL on macOS using Homebrew. Learn how to set up the database server, create users, and connect to your local instance.
  Keywords: install PostgreSQL macOS, PostgreSQL setup, Homebrew postgres
- [SQL Countries Table Example: How to Create and Query Efficiently](https://shramko.dev/snippets/countries-sql): Ready-to-use SQL insert statements for a countries table with names, ISO codes, and phone codes. Perfect for seeding your database with real country data.
